reviews.review_text: One of the most beautiful games I have ever played. The combat is unique and the lore feels genuine to the culture.
great game and probably my favorite story game ever but after Sony announced they will not be releasing ghost of Yotei or any other single player games to pc i Sony lost my support

Great story, gameplay, and awesome open world. 
This game will be one of the classics.

Very nice and not too hard of a game. Not really a souls game but you could say its a good starting point as it doesnt punish you for each death unlike most souls game. Graphics are insanely good during its time and its still beautiful on 2026. New Game+ features are nice too if you are looking to grind into 100+ hours of this game.
Great game, the story structure, the combat systems, and the graphics are combined to create something absolutely amazing. Must play for story game lovers.

This game not only one the most beautiful games,  it's also one of the most immersive Samurai games. You really dissolve yourself into Jin Sakai story, his choices and Japanese culture. I haven't been really into it before this game and planning my first trip to Japan right after. It was an amazing experience. Thank you!

Barring a Ghost of Yotei PC release, and assuming that the Assassins Creed franchise fails to ever get their sh*t together, Ghost of Tsushima is most likely the best Samurai RPG game you can play on PC, or arguably any gaming platform. I didn't even want to play the game until I got a computer that could adequately run it the way I wanted to run it, and when I finally was able to make that happen, my goodness. 

Pro #1 = Visually AMAZING. This game has a photo mode built into it and you can easily tell why. Environments, scenery, and action are second to none when it comes to beauty, and there are a good variety of environments that themselves all look stunning. Flowering springs, winter snows, imposing fortifications and castles, scorched earth terrain... It all is a visual treat to behold from the beginning of the game to the end. Ghost of Tsushima's open world is as beautiful as Red Dead Redemption 2's is realistic (and still beautiful too, dont get me wrong)

Pro #2 = Story missions and side missions are engaging... If you asked a game studio to make a samurai game, you could probably guess about 5-10 run-of-the-mill predictable tropes or story missions they would put in the game. There were multiple times where this did NOT happen in Ghost of Tsushima, where according to my fiance I said "wait what the ♥♥♥♥" at least 3 times because I was expecting missions to go one way, but then they took a HARD u-turn. Betrayals, revelations, motivations, a handful of war crimes committed by a monk, this game does a solid job of subverting expectations of where you think the story goes, vs what actually takes place, without seeming wildly over the top or out of character based on whats going on. I was invested in the story throughout the game, Im sure you will too. 

====

Regarding the combat system in the game, let me preface this by saying my approach to combat in video games is 'basic ♥♥♥♥♥'. I prefer button mashing and expecting the enemy to go down in 2-3 hits, and if that doesnt happen or if my aim is off Im gonna have a bad time. I played the game on easy mode and a few times was getting manhandled by enemy npc's that on a higher difficulty would have killed me quickly, so if you DONT have much ability with combat games then you may need to brace yourself with this game. I fully acknowledge I may actually have a skill issue with that, it probably also didnt help that I picked armor that barely offered protection because I wanted the armor that unlocked more of the map and identified items easier... You are gonna have to brush up on your combat skills if you want to proceed through the game, and if an idiot like me was able to get it done, you probably can too if you are still reading this. 

10/10 game. We're not gonna get something like this for a while, and even if we do, it will live under the shadow of this one.
Good combat, excellent story, stunning graphics!

This game is an absolute masterpiece. I cannot express the love I have for this game, in words. This is my second time around playing it, and on PC instead of PS5, and somehow the game is EVEN BETTER. The story is never dull, the constant action is amazing, the development of not only your character but others as you progress throughout the island is amazing. This game is worth ALL the money. If you haven't played yet, DO IT. You will NOT regret one second of game play.

Ghost of Tsushima is a must-play. Amazing experience!

What it does well:
-Outstanding visuals. The color palette is fantastic. What it lacks in some texture quality, it more than makes up for in atmosphere, draw distance and art style.
-The Story is very well written and emotional.
-The Combat and builds are so much fun.
-The DLC included is awesome. Spices up gameplay, has a tight story & exploration is short and sweet.
-Cool cosmetics.

What it doesn't do well:
-The Side activities are very repetitive, and there is a lot of them. Most are needed for full completion.
-The side missions, in my opinion, are mostly drawn out and boring. (Not every one, but most.)
-A minor thing is the menus. The cosmetic sorting is abysmal. Once you have lots of items, you are forced to scroll through 50+ items which are sorted in seemingly no order to find what you want. 
-The DLC is available WAY too early. The DLC clearly takes place after the mains story, but is available to do 33% into it. Why? No clue.
-While the combat is amazing, it is essentially the only gameplay here. There's nothing to do other than walk around & explore, if you don't want to kill people, lol. Compare that to RDR2, lot's of side activities that are fun!


In my opinion, a solid 8.5 out of 10. A must play for enjoyers of Japanese culture.

All my hours for this game are on the PS5, ended up getting it again on PC because it's one of my favorite games.  The atmosphere, storywriting, and gameplay loop are all refined very well.  I write this review after having played Ghost of Yotei and completing it on the PS5 as well.  As the newer game, its graphics and polish are greatly improved from this title, but Tsushima maintains a superior story.  The narrative made me cry several times, and I am very endeared by Jin as a main character.  Following his struggles between his family's samurai ethics and the efficacy of "dishonorable" tactics make his character development through the game very interesting to follow, especially as an Assassin's Creed fan.  
Also, love the outfits and environmental interactions (wind, mud, plants, blood splatter, etc.)  While this may not have the same polish as Yotei nor the overhauls to exploration and completion,  Jin laps Atsu several times around the sun as a protagonist that is enjoyable and relatable.
I love the game itself, but i'm boycotting sony, we were months away from getting yotei on pc, but sony has gotta be gay

[h1]The Best Assassin's Creed Game That Never Was[/h1]

One of the best, if not THE best, take on the Assassin's Creed open world formula, which both hurts and helps Ghost of Tsushima.

The combat is flat out the best of the best. It's not a spam fest where you can power through twelve enemies by button mashing; you are rewarded for being patient and parrying. The environments and lighting are taken a step further with a unique art style that's not hyper-realistic, but not cartoony either.

The only real downside to Ghost of Tsushima's being the best Assassin's Creed game that never was is that, similar to most Assassin's Creed games, some of Ghost of Tsushima's characters become quite forgettable and flat. There are exceptions, of course, but the bulk of the side characters fail to leave a lasting impression.

[h1]9/10[/h1]
Eh, pretty boring game. Would rather just watch the storyline as a movie.

Tried for the 2nd time to finish it and I did not. GoT starts out strong but once I got to the part where the upper island oppens up I already lost motivation. Sounds exactly like the averege Ubisoft experience where the first part of the map, you want to 100% it but you feel the motivation lessen during that attempt.

Combat feels good but the controlls for bow and throwables are weird although servicable, even after around 35 hours with them, 

The story isn't captivating enough which makes the fact of not having SKIP CUTSCENE highly annoying.

It isn't a bad game. Its just a basic Ubisoft game without Ubisoft and with some more care here and there. The avarege open world affair.

Well, this game tries so hard to sell itself as a stylized samurai experience and it surely did it some justice but I recommend it with real caution or lets say I don't recommend it at full price. In essence, it is an Ubisoft-esque beautiful-to-look-at but bloated open world, an Assassin's Creed clone with more emphasis set on the sword/katana combat and less emphasis on platforming yet you can still play stealth at least to some point. There are some other options of the engagement like half bow archery or kunai throwables whilst the game usually forces you to katana combat. This is understandable from a pop culture samurai narrative perspective but it gets repetitive fast and stealth mechanics are mediocre at best. The animations are of high quality but it is insufficient to carry the entire game like that. The gameplay lacks depth despite it attempts to sell itself otherwise but it is just a little bit more engaging version of classical animation driven counter melee/flight combat of classical AC series with some hitboxes added. 

While in some departments and headlines, it is worse than Ubisoft/AC games. For instance, the amount of assets are relatively less compared to many AC games and you may sometimes feel there are just copy paste filler assets everywhere or no assets at all, just bare empiteness and boring to traverse large open spaces. It starts to get consuming with the repetitive gameplay loop and this feeling strucks faster here compared to the more content-asset rich AC titles. Compared to some AC games, the lack of side content cinematics most part further exacerbates the problem and this is one of the serious issues of this game. The characters and camera usually stand still in every dialogue just their mouth and barely arms or hands moving. It is a terrible presentation in a game that puts such emphasis on the quality of in-game animations, a terrible choice for a story that takes itself so serious as a cinematic experience. Some questlines are way too long, gameplay content wise repetitive or barebones which are effectively undermined with those lack of animated cinematics I have talked about.

The last but not least, the main story looks and feels pretty linear and compact which this bloated open world design just does not do justice. The characters are pretty generic including the main guy Jin Sakai, like they are not terrible but nothing special. In general, it does not feel like it is worth to finish this game bc it is a dragged open world.

If my description fits your taste in some parts like flight animation type (but as I say unfortunately repetitive) katana combat and if you are eager to ignore the flaws I have mentioned so far, then you may give it a chance on a sale. For me, it could be an easily hard pass. It is an overrated Assassins Creed hardcoded/variant game. In some departments, it is better than AC; in some, it is worse than AC. I believe it exploits of being Sony title. If it had been an Ubisoft title, people would have evaluated it under much more negative light. Sony/Playstation brand bias does a considerable lifting here, elevating a 5-7/10 mid game to 8-9/10 "masterpiece".
Beautiful world, addictive combat, but dragged down by repetitive “Ubisoft-style” design and forgettable characters — worth one playthrough, just don’t expect depth.

Simply amazing, old gamer head here, big fan of Assassins Creed. Either I've played too many ACs or this game simply does things better, the story, visuals, track, gameplay and side quests are all interesting and I found myself wasting time completing each map's side quests before progressing the main story because the world is immersive and the lore is captivating. Definitely recommend. Also I play it on the Steam Deck, runs at 30 fps but no visual lag or anything that interrupts gameplay, especially in combat or during sensitive missions, mainly when exploring the open world on the horse.
Pretty good game, combat is fun, story is entertaining, would recommend

I have to say as someone who has never really been all into samurai or Japanese culture as a hole this game is a dang masterpiece. the graphics are probably the best i have ever seen, the game play is so much fun, and the story will is one of my top 10 in all of gaming, the charters are also really well written.

PS. runs really well on deck!
love this style of game. Good story, lots to do. Good exploration
one of the most scenic and interesting games ive ever played. 11-10 would highly recommend
I'll echo the disdain of others for not being able to skip cutscenes... aside from that, I enjoyed the game a lot. Solid story, fun combat, plenty of open world to explore.
Amazing graphics (with good performance), good fights and story. What need more for a perfect game?

dramatic ahh ending (loved it). amazing storyline and world building, you can't get bored honestly. breathtaking visuals, even with an old ahh gpu, i boot it up once in a while just to run around the scenery

Absolute masterpiece, everything about this game is stellar. if you want to feel like a samurai/ronin without going out of your way to begin studying kendo and iaido. although I also recommend taking up Hema and Japanese armed martial arts.

Genuinely one of the best games I've ever played. It's in my top 10 easily, probably in my top 3. The story is beautifully and well written. The gameplay is fluid, functional, and responsive. The controls are simple but give you so many tools for fighting your enemies. The sword combat is deceptively simple. You can do so much without needing to remember a million combos. 11/10 no notes, would and will play again.
Beautiful open world with satisfying sword combat and a well written story. Would recommend.
goat of tsushima. this game is so lovingly made. attention to detail is incredible. great characters, satisfying theme and gameplay, and the two are inextricably woven together

Really great game.
Insane combat.
Visually stunning.
Lacks a bit of finesse. (compared to the goat RDR2)
It's not a modern classic for nothing...

ps: I was able to play with 100 fps in medium to high settings in 1440p

This game is just amazing, the combat, the graphics, the story, the characters, and the vast map, ect. This game is just so well put together and the way the story plays out is great and the main character Jin is the perfect anti hero and the way every character has their own impact on the story and game play makes this game one of my favorite single player action packed game and the stealth in this game is not a let down and i would really recommend this game to anyone who has never played it or is considering buying in again.

Lately, in my opinion, there have been too many games about Japanese or Chinese historical settings, and it’s starting to get a bit repetitive since the stories are mostly the same. However, I can’t say I didn’t like this game at all.

At the beginning, you play as a samurai who has to save the island and his uncle from the Mongols on his own, but gradually he starts turning into something like a ghost ninja, which is basically the opposite of traditional samurai values. And that’s actually what makes this game stand out compared to other games set in medieval Asia, especially Japan.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Останнім часом, як на мене, стало занадто багато ігор на японську або китайську історичну тематику, і це вже починає трохи набридати, бо сюжети в них практично однакові. Однак не можу сказати, що ця гра мені зовсім не сподобалася.

Спочатку ти граєш за самурая, який має самотужки врятувати острів і свого дядька від монголів, але поступово він починає перетворюватися на щось схоже на ніндзя-привида, тобто повну протилежність канонам самурая. І в цьому є певна родзинка саме цієї гри, на відміну від інших про середньовічну Азію, а саме Японію.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

最近は、日本や中国の歴史をテーマにしたゲームが多すぎるように感じていて、ストーリーもほとんど同じなので少し飽きてきています。しかし、このゲームが全く気に入らなかったというわけではありません。

最初は、モンゴルから島と叔父を一人で救うサムライとしてプレイしますが、徐々にゴーストのような忍者へと変わっていきます。これはサムライの本来の価値観とは正反対の存在であり、そこにこのゲームならではの面白さがあります。他の中世アジア、特に日本を舞台にしたゲームとは違う魅力だと思います。

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

В последнее время, как по мне, стало слишком много игр на японскую или китайскую историческую тематику, и это уже начинает немного надоедать, потому что сюжеты в них практически одинаковые. Однако не могу сказать, что эта игра мне совсем не не понравилась.

Сначала ты играешь за самурая, который должен в одиночку спасти остров и своего дядю от монголов, но постепенно он начинает превращаться в нечто вроде ниндзя-призрака, то есть полную противоположность канонам самурая. И в этом есть своя изюминка именно этой игры, в отличие от других проектов про средневековую Азию, а именно Японию.

172 hours played.

I completed the entire story twice on Hard difficulty, and Ghost of Tsushima is one of the few games that kept me hooked all the way through both playthroughs.

The combat is excellent and never feels repetitive. Fighting on Hard makes every duel more rewarding, and mastering the different stances feels satisfying. Whether I was charging into battle as a samurai or using stealth tactics as the Ghost, the gameplay stayed fun even after hundreds of fights.

What really stood out to me was the story and the characters. The twists hit hard, especially learning that Lady Masako's own sister was involved in the massacre of the Adachi family. Moments like that made the side stories feel just as important as the main plot.

The world of Tsushima is beautiful, and exploration never felt like a chore. Visiting temples, shrines, hot springs, bamboo strikes, and hidden locations was genuinely enjoyable. I especially loved the Shinto Shrines that required climbing and parkour to reach. Reaching the top after navigating narrow paths, cliffs, and jumps always felt rewarding, and the views from the shrines were often breathtaking. Instead of feeling like a checklist, exploration felt like an adventure.

After 172 hours and two full playthroughs, I can easily say this is one of the best open-world games I've played. Amazing combat, memorable characters, a fantastic story, and a world that is worth getting lost in.

Highly recommended.

Beautiful. The visuals of the Japanese landscapes are definitely a big feature in this game. Accompanied with some amazingly cinematic moments and a unique combat system that does a great job relating the mechanics to the story and the character you are playing. All I have is a gaming laptop which only has so much processing power when it comes to graphics, but even on the lowest settings the game looks great.

10/10 this game is amazing. I don't think I'd change anything about it. It's beautiful and has a great balance of action and story which are both top notch. Good progression and freedom to choose your path a bit. Loved every minute of it.

Sony is no longer supporting PC as a platform for it's single player games, So I give this a negative review not because of the game itself as it is truley amazing. But for the fact the Sony is once again alienating a huge demographic for either self-serving, monitization, or lazy reasons.

It's sad GoT had to be hit in the crossfire, but knowing i'll never be able to play the sequal without shilling out multiple hundreds of dollars for a console I don't have a single game for currently, does indeed affect the experience playing the game.
Satisfying combat and a powerful story. Ghost of Tsushima makes you question what honor truly means, proving that it's not always black and white. Are you honorable enough to play this game?

peak. love this game a lot and wish i could play yotei but i dont have a ps. 
the story was quite cool but a bit confusing? gameplay was peak though and if you have a good (great) pc you can play on medium graphics, if you dont then uhhhh tough luck ig

with my specs i ran a solid 80-90 fps on medium graphics
Very good game Loved the story, characters and the combat would play again in the future
Finished first act. Pretty descent game, but not my type, i think. It's really beautiful and atmospheric, but i didn't find reason to finish it completely. Maybe i'll return to it later.

Nice game and a good story......
I would recommend it and out of 10 , I'll give 8.8 stars
Samurai fighting the Mongol Empire to reclaim Japan simulator. Incredible world building and gameplay. The story is a bit of a slow burn, but it's definitely a rewarding and heartfelt narrative.

W story
W music
W gameplay
W visuals


"this horse will be with you for your entire journey"

Amazing story, graphics, and open world game. 

Truly one of my favorite games of all time!
5/5 bet game if you love some free story game that you can go anywhere and all is in japan culture especially if you have a great PC the grafic its nicee

I was so glad to finally play Ghost of Tsushima as a PC player because this open-world game genuinely feels different from others in the same genre.

First of all, I highly recommend playing on the hardest difficulty. Not only do you experience a more accurate presentation of how dangerous the Mongols actually were during that time, but it also motivates you to wisely use every piece of equipment you have. This difficulty also forces you at the beginning of the game to avoid direct encounters with the enemy as much as possible, which totally fits the situation of starting as a surviving samurai who lost most of his gear. But don't worry, as you progress further and further, this difficulty will actually feel normal in my experience.

The combat itself can be compared to the Assassin's Creed games, especially the newer ones with less focus on parkour. Still, Ghost of Tsushima definitely has enough features to be a unique experience. My favorite one is the option to start a duel on enemy contact if you don't want to use stealth. Not only is this super immersive for the setting, but it also turns a direct encounter into a strategic moment. Also, the "fear feature" where enemies see you as an untouchable samurai and start running away because they are too scared, is incredibly immersive. It makes the combat feel like an actual fight rather than just harvesting deaths from one soulless warrior to another. Especially your "ultimate," which lets you one-hit multiple enemies without resistance, is awesome. Still, what I don't like about the combat are the different sword stances. The reason is that they function more like a rock-paper-scissors principle. There is only one correct stance for each enemy type, which makes the combat feel more monotonous rather than extra tactical.

In terms of general exploration, there is a lot to do in the open world, and surprisingly, every single activity is highly enjoyable. I would say that I "accidentally" completed all side activities, whether it was writing my own personal haikus or engaging in the challenging duels. I didn't feel any grind at all, even when tracking down every armor piece or weapon, because all of them remained useful until the end due to their unique perks. All of this motivated me to keep playing even after more than 50 hours of playtime, which is an achievement that is super hard to pull off, and I really respect that. There isn't anything particular I can criticize about the open world except that they messed up the great wind navigation. Unlike on the PlayStation controller, you need to use a trigger button and the stick simultaneously, which feels very intricate when you are riding a horse.

The story overall was very enjoyable. Instead of being just a generic revenge plot with the cliché honor tradition of Japanese culture, they actually go deeper by showing both sides, especially in the Iki Island expansion.

All in all, Ghost of Tsushima is, in my opinion, an open-world masterpiece because it does something super important that too many other games in the genre forget: a player should explore a world to actually enjoy the activities in there, rather than just to grind. Don't create infinite side quests or anything that artificially tries to extend playtime. Instead, try to make a game that shines through what it actually has to offer. So, if you like open-world games but prefer quality over quantity, then Ghost of Tsushima gets my absolute recommendation.

Kind of your standard asscreedemup but with tight combat and a pretty simple thematic hook: the tension between the code of the samurai and the reality of what protecting your subjects from the Mongols will require. Where it excels though is the ways in which it guides you through the game world in more immersive ways than the proverbial quest marker. It's not perfect; the wind should definitely be toned down in intensity, and the birds and foxes are pretty repetitive after a point, but they're great ideas to build upon to make open game worlds more interesting to explore.
Amazing game. It is beautiful to look at and explore and the combat is fun. The story and characters are amazing and will be putting in the hours to have it 100% done.
why the hell would they update the game and get rid of the in game control schemes now I gotta use ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ steam input and be ass at the game wtf.
Great game, wish the 2nd one would come out on PC. The combat was fluid and fantastic and the game ran well unlike some ports.
The graphics are super, combined with strong story telling and atomic tales  this game is the best i have everplayed.
Just Perfection. It's perfectly optimized too. Hands Down one of the best games released in this decade!

Ghost of Tsushima is the best game I have ever played. Every thing about it; art, atmospheric vibes, combat, stealth. The cut scenes are superior and flow so cinematically it doesn't even seem like a cut scene. The wind guide at first was a challenge but it is now one of my most favorite features of the game and forces the player to use their context clues and environmental cues. I would love to have the first original experience of this game again if I could. 10/10, 5 stars. All of the above!

8.5/10
Amazing game from start to finish and every aspect. The biggest issues I have with this game are graphical bugs with fog/wind and constant stuttering after playing for a couple hours. I doubt it's ever gonna get fixed since Sony is cancelling their PC ports. If it wasn't for that I'd give it a 9/10.

Note: Played the full game on Fedora 43 on Proton Experimental so I don't know if playing on windows would be more/less stable.

This game has been one of the few story games that i have genuelly enjoyed playing and completing most of the achievements, and been stunned by the beauty of the island that the game has to offer.

The gameplay is both fun and challenging, making you learn new moves that are both overpowered and incredibly cool to act out, the different ways you are able to take bases down and sneak into them can truly make you feel like you are playing as a samurai or as a ghost. 

the story overall is pretty predictable but there are many points in the game where the narrative can both surprise you and make you emotional (yes i cried atleast 2 times playing this).

overall pretty good game and good story i would give it a 8/10.
Amazing storyline and cinematic gameplay.
Very nice atmosphere, Very deep story, and it catches you for hours. Sometimes it become a bit boring in side quests, but as a whole, its one of the best games I've ever played.

Ghost of Tsushima is hands down one of the most beautiful games ever made. The world feels alive, the combat is smooth and satisfying, and the story is absolutely incredible. Jin Sakai’s journey from samurai to the Ghost is emotional, brutal, and unforgettable. Every mission, every duel, and every quiet moment in the open world feels meaningful. This game isn’t just fun to play — it’s an experience that stays with you long after the credits roll.
Brilliant story, great combat, possibly the best game I have played on PC

As someone who never owned a PlayStation, it was bittersweet hearing how incredible Ghost of Tsushima was and knowing I’d probably never get to play it. So when it finally arrived on Steam, I bought it instantly and it absolutely lived up to the hype. The game feels like a blend of Far Cry and Assassin’s Creed, set in a beautifully realized samurai era Japan. The combat, armor progression, skills, and story are all top tier. Even the unskippable cutscenes work in the game’s favor, pulling you deeper into the world. A fantastic experience all around. Highly recommended.
Great game, side quests are repetitive if you are trying to get the 100% for this game. That being said the bamboo strikes are the goat

This game is an absolute masterpiece. Even after six hours in, I know it's going to be one of my favorite games ever.

This game is ~crazy~ beautiful. Runs like a dream.

Baku the Voiceless was the true highlight of this game. Lmaooo. His cursed charms made everything truly interesting. 🤣 

Peak graphics, peak story. Tho the Mongolian's DOSHO DOSHO annoyed me for some reason. Just made me wanna murder them even more. 

Iki Island was a delight, too. The 7 second archery challenge gave me good memories on wasting newly glued acrylic nails because getting gold is absolutely important. Lmao. 

Love it. GoY port soon please.
Fantastic graphics all round, Peak story, Amazing gameplay and cutscene's

I recommend this game with one caveat - have time to play this out.  The cutscenes are painfully unskippable and there are so many repeating cutscenes that it will annoy you at some point.  I hear you can skip many in Game+, but it took me 246 hours to completely liberate all areas (including Iki Island, which was my favorite part).

This was definitely a fun and fulfilling game.  Graphics are beautiful, there might have been one standoff bug where the camera angle prevented me from seeing the attacker, but out of 246 hours, that's pretty damn good.

Slow story.  Slow progress.  Slow game.  Highly recommend.
Fun game, beautiful landscape, definitely worth playing

Absolutely incredible environment, even better coAbsolutely incredible environment, even better combat, and one of the most beautiful open worlds ever made. It completely nails both the story and exploration. Highly recommended. Play the damn gamembat, and one of the most beautiful open worlds ever made. It completely nails both the story and exploration. Massively recommended.

as a strong japanese samurai warrior in real life who has fought multiple battles for her country i rlly feel represented in this game
awesome

This is without a doubt, one of the most incredible gaming experiences I have ever had. I will think about this game until I die, that's how magical it was for me. The commitment to the game's setting makes it feel alive. The graphics and art style are just stunning and serve the story. On top of that, this has to be the most satisfying combat I've ever played. Exploration doesn't feel like a chore - there are so many unique and beautifully created environments on the island that it's one of the few adventure games I have ever played where I actually felt compelled to explore and see as much I could rather than just burn through the story. The characters are well developed and there are very few 'stock archetypes'. 

I really can't overstate how much of all-encompassing and beautiful world this is, even though the story contrasts this with the horrors of a violent, foreign invasion. This is truly one of the most special games I have ever played.

[h2]Ghost of Tsushima Director’s Cut Review[/h2]

[b]Ghost of Tsushima Director’s Cut[/b] is an incredible game that blends beautiful visuals, emotional storytelling, and satisfying combat into one unforgettable experience. The island of Tsushima feels alive, with stunning landscapes, peaceful exploration, and intense battles that make every moment feel cinematic.

The combat is one of the best parts of the game. Sword fighting feels smooth and rewarding, while stealth gives you the freedom to approach enemies in your own way. Jin Sakai’s story is also very powerful, as he struggles between honor, duty, and doing whatever it takes to protect his home.

The Director’s Cut makes the experience even better with the Iki Island expansion, adding more story, challenges, and emotional depth. Overall, [b]Ghost of Tsushima Director’s Cut[/b] is a beautiful, polished, and memorable game that is absolutely worth playing.

[b]Rating:[/b] 10/10
Really enjoying this game.  Reminds me of the great fun I have had with RDR2, Horizon Zero Dawn and Horizon Forbidden West.
The game is amazing. Extremely beautiful from every angle. Satisfying and rewarding combat system and a cinematic story line
a really fun stealth  action game with good story telling

Ghost of Tsushima is incredible, I loved every second of my playthrough. The combat is extremely satisfying, the story is phenomenal with stunning visuals and great cinematic scenes. Its the best samurai game I've ever played, I highly recommend it.

It just gets better on the second try.

Its just great, the side quest and the sheer scale. There are some things off, and I would recommend searching the internet to keep the timeline straight, but uts amazing

Great game. Beautiful graphics and scenes. Satisfying combat. Story kept me invested, that said, I won't say it's the greatest ever. Can get monotonous at times but worth pushing through those moments. I'm very hard to please when it comes to games these days so it says a lot that I kept wanting to play more and more.

Worth it. This game makes me late. Late to work. Late to dinner. Late to bed. Sometimes I start to play and find half the day is gone. Whether you play as the stealth archer or just the tank swordsman, this game will scratch your itch. And I have a crappy computer, no issues running this. On top of the gameplay, the game is beautiful. You can spend hours just admiring the scenery or riding your horse across grasslands through flocks of birds. One of the few games I should have paid full price for.

Pros
- Visually 1 of the nicest looking games I've ever played. 1 of only 2 games that I took multiple screenshots in. So many locations you can pause and just admire the scenery.
- Side characters are great, Lady Masako, Sensei Ishikawa, Yuna, Kenji, and Norio. They were all different and interesting in their own ways, their side missions were some of the best missions in the game.
- The combat while easy and kind of repetitive, it's still very nice and flows well. You can also play in a few different styles
- The DLC on Iki Island was great, the enemies were slightly harder than on the main island, and I also found the Eagle to be a much better villain over the Khan.
- All the cosmetic choices are nice, different armor dyes, kitana, masks, and head gear to choose from. Can look and fight the way you want
- Overall the side missions were decent, some better than others, but a couple of real gems in there to be found.

Cons
- The main story is fairly predictable, can figure out the ending before the halfway point of the main story.
- While the combat is still fun, it did get fairly easy, I played on hard and probably should have played on lethal.
-Minor nitpick but the charms are mostly useless, so it feels like doing the fox dens are pointless, but the foxes are cute so whatever.
- Khan the villain in the main story is pretty generic, some obese, arrogant, and boring Mongol villain, Didn't find anything about him fun or interesting. The villain in the DLC the Eagle was way better in my opinion.
- Wish the ending tied the characters together a bit better.
- DLC becomes available about mid game, but I'd recommend waiting until after the main story.

Overall great game, I would definitely recommend this game. Solid characters, beautiful looking world, fun but easy gameplay (recommend playing on lethal difficulty), can explore different playstyles and look cool while doing it. 8.5/10

I finally finished my first playthrough of Ghost of Tsushima, and while I still have a few achievements left for 100%, I really enjoyed the overall experience. You play as a samurai raised on Tsushima Island during the Mongol invasion, and as the story progresses you’re forced to choose between traditional samurai honor and more “unorthodox” methods. The game presents this as a moral conflict, but in my opinion the story doesn’t actually change based on your choices or dialogue options. The only moment where I felt I had a real choice was on Iki Island, which made the rest feel a bit predetermined.

The open world is beautiful, with many areas that genuinely impressed me, though some parts felt a bit dull or empty. One thing that bothered me was the climbing system — you can only climb at specific points, which becomes frustrating in a world with so many cliffs and elevations.

Combat is engaging and challenging, especially when facing multiple enemies with different weapons and attack patterns. Switching stances is essential, but using an Xbox controller caused issues for me. The same button used to change stances is also used to pick up dropped items, so my character kept grabbing loot instead of switching stance mid‑fight. When you’re fighting a spear enemy while stuck in the wrong stance, it gets annoying fast.

Since I started playing, I haven’t received any updates, so I assume the game is no longer supported. I did encounter a few bugs, which is disappointing for a game of this quality.

Despite the flaws, I enjoyed experiencing this period through the game. The themes of honor, loyalty, betrayal, and the harsh reality of war are well presented. It shows how people change, how they suffer, and how war affects even those who aren’t fighting. It really highlights the beauty of the island and the destruction caused by conflict.

The story may not react to your choices, but liberating the island gives you freedom in how you approach each encounter. So the real question becomes: will you fight with honor, or strike from the shadows?

it is a good game overall the story kinda carries cause its crazy good the combat is alright but has a problem of being too easy if you just do all the side quest and that is another problem that some side quests are boring and repetitive but except for that i think its a great game and it is story heavy and they did it well so thats the big thing

Truly one of the greats, one of the greatest games to ever be released. This game has the best features a game can offer at a perfectly moderate amount. Every aspect is perfectly carved out, refined, and perfected within the games world. One of the most immersive, lifelike open worlds ive ever played, the scenery is breathtaking enough for you to stop what youre doing and admire the scenery. the combat is perfect, flawless in every way, every sword strike is perfectly timed, every block is exactly when you decided to block, just when youve started to drift away a new aspect is thrown in your face. every story is perfect, balanced, human. This is a game that deserves the title as one of the Greatest of all time, 10/10 i recommend it to everyone i meet, long live the ghost, i will never forget this game.

One of the best open world games I've played, and the combat is very smooth. The main story is weak, and most of the characters outside of Jin Sakai (main protagonist), Lord Shimura, and Yuna are forgettable and have a weak writing. If you enjoy a beautiful map clearing game, this is the one.

Samurai Simulator


Thought it was very pretentious at first, then as I kept playing the story got way more enthralling, the combat is mad difficult though, takes some getting used to, other than that, excited to finish it.

This is a great game with a fantastic story, very emotional and moving in both it's imagery, score, and setting. Visually it's one of the most breathtaking games with deep respect for Japanese mythology and culture. Historically it's loosely based on the Mongol invasion with a more romanticized take, but it doesn't detract at all from the story they're trying to tell or break immersion. Highly recommend to anyone who loves classic samurai films, there's a Kurosawa mode! I've played it multiple times all the way through, both on my PS5 and again on Steam. Enjoy!

LOVED IT! Such a beautiful game! I've never spent so much time in photo-mode before playing this, it's that visually stunning! The main and side stories were so well done and heart-wrenching. This is definitely one of my favorite open world games alongside the Witcher 3 and RDR2.
amazing game, beautiful fun, charasmatic.

Stunningly beautiful game. Fantastic story and character building. Combat is incredibly customizable and promotes creativity. Everything I disliked about certain games in the past were elegantly solved in this game creating a smooth and enjoyable playthrough I can't relate to any other game. Hats off to devs making this steam deck compatible. 10/10 would recommend
Great story, characters, and gameplay. Sword fighting feels rewarding as does exploration. Looks incredible! You absolutely won't regret getting this one!
Really good story, love the gameplay, good game mechanic. 10/10

Are you a slave to honor? Probably not. This game is pretty enjoyable, from music to combat. It feels fun to stealth around and kill one by one. or to bait crowds of enemies and just bomb them from the roof tops. All poison is good poison and this is some good ass poison.

Ghost of Tsushima is an absolutely beautiful game with one of the best open worlds I’ve ever seen. The visuals, atmosphere, music, and overall vibe are incredible, and exploring the island feels amazing. The story and cinematic moments are also really well done.

That said, the combat started feeling repetitive after a while and sometimes a bit clunky, especially during longer play sessions. It looks stylish, but the gameplay loop doesn’t evolve enough later in the game. Also got hit with the classic PlayStation curse since multiplayer isn’t available in my region, which was really disappointing because Legends mode looked fun.

Overall still a great game and worth playing for the world and experience alone.

A stunning game. Visuals are insane. I recommend playing this late at night with headphones, let your life go and take in the samurai role. Music is amazing. The combat is the best I've ever seen. I can't say enough how good this game looks and the combat is just so good! Please play this game!
10/10
This is one of the greatest games i have ever played. The graphics are incredible and the story is phenominal. wish i could play again for the first time.
Great graphics and gameplay, the story is compelling and the characters are well fleshed out. A great game.

This is the first samurai game that I have played. At first, I was afraid that the combat might be too Soulslike , as I am not a big fan of those types of games. However, I did not have any difficulty in picking up the different stances and becoming a badass samurai sword master. The story is an absolute Epic Samurai tale.  In short, I  liked this game so much that I bought a PS5 just to play the sequel.

Play the game you will not regret it, if you like those types of games.
Nice story, characters and graphics and game mechanics. Can not say anything bad about the game.
